text.title: django-quality-logging Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-quality-logging as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Mixing structlog.get_logger() and logging.getLogger() in the same module.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without ProcessorFormatter bridging them, half the logs from the module are unstructured plain text. The bridge requires explicit configuration. Pick one per module: use structlog everywhere for new code.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Full transaction tracing at 1.0 captures every request and can cost 10x the base Sentry bill on high-traffic services. Start at 0.01 and increase based on cost tolerance.

failure-mode.description: Sentry before_send filter that drops entire events to avoid PII.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Dropping events to avoid PII removes the error context entirely. Scrub specific fields (password, token) from the event dict instead of returning None.

failure-mode.description: django-silk profiling every request in production at default settings.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: django-silk stores every request's SQL queries and metadata to the database by default. Without SILKY_INTERCEPT_PERCENT = 1 (1% sampling), it doubles the database write load and fills disk.

failure-mode.description: Logging DEBUG-level SQL queries in production (django.db.backends at DEBUG).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every ORM query generates a DEBUG log event. In a view issuing 10 queries to serve 1000 req/s, that is 10,000 log events/second — saturating log pipelines and causing log drop.
